buffer_overflow.description: gets() has no bounds check — always exploitable. Replace with fgets()
strcpy() has no bounds check — use strlcpy() or strncpy() with length
strcat() has no bounds check — use strlcat() or strncat() with remaining size
sprintf() has no bounds check — use snprintf() with explicit size
vsprintf() has no bounds check — use vsnprintf()
scanf %s without field width — unbounded string read. Use %Ns with explicit size
String comparison — verify both strings are properly null-terminated
memcpy with computed size — verify destination buffer is at least that large
alloca() allocates on stack — large or user-controlled size causes stack overflow
Wide-string copy/cat without bounds — use wcslcpy/wcslcat or wcsncpy/wcsncat
Fixed-size buffer with potential overflow from read function

format_string.description: printf() with single variable argument — format string injection. Use printf("%s", var)
fprintf() with variable as format string
sprintf() with variable as format string
syslog() with variable as format string
err()/warn() with variable as format string

integer_overflow.description: malloc(n * sizeof) — integer overflow if n is large. Use calloc() or check for overflow before multiply
malloc with addition — verify no integer overflow on size
realloc with multiply in size argument — integer overflow risk
calloc(n, sizeof) — verify n is validated before call

use_after_free.description: Variable used after free — verify pointer is set to NULL after free()
Multiple free() calls close together — check for double-free
free() not followed by NULL assignment — dangling pointer risk

null_pointer.description: malloc() return value not checked for NULL before use
realloc() return value not checked — on failure returns NULL, original pointer lost
fopen() return value not checked for NULL

race_condition.description: TOCTOU: access() check then open() — race condition window
TOCTOU: stat() check then open() — race condition window
tmpnam()/tempnam() — TOCTOU race. Use mkstemp() instead

dangerous_functions.description: system() — verify argument is not user-controlled. Prefer execv() family
popen() — shell injection if argument contains user input
exec() with shell — verify arguments are not user-controlled
Dropping to root UID/GID — verify this is intentional
crypt() with non-modern hash prefix — verify using $6$ (SHA-512) or stronger
